codeReview.summary: The miri-analysis feature updated .factory/library/miri-compatibility.md with comprehensive Miri results. The worker ran Miri on all 4 new M1 bug-fix test files (channel_create_leak, sched_context_create_leak, handle_send_rollback, channel_create_pte_leak — 22 tests, all clean) plus critical files (heap, slab, syscall, heap_routing, oom, adversarial_slab, adversarial_buddy, cross_module). Zero kernel UB found. Two binaries (adversarial_stress, adversarial_vma) correctly skipped for timeout. The assembly-dependent blind spots section is thorough — 34 inline asm blocks across 7 categories with per-category risk assessment and test model coverage analysis. The actual Miri analysis work is solid and all 4 validation contract assertions (VAL-MIRI-001 through VAL-MIRI-004) are fulfilled.

issues.description: Header claims '685/965 tests pass' but the per-file table sums to 893 passed tests across non-skipped binaries. The discrepancy of 208 tests suggests the header was computed differently from the table (possibly counting only tests the worker actually ran under Miri in this session plus the previous run's 555, rather than the table totals). The per-file table appears internally consistent (893 pass + 27 ignored + 45 skipped = 965 total).
Header claims '26 tests ignored' but the ignored tests table lists 27: adversarial_buddy (1) + buddy (1) + ipc (24) + oom (1) = 27. Off by 1.

Header claims '31 verified test binaries' but 35 total minus 2 skipped = 33 non-skipped binaries. The table presents results for all 33 non-skipped binaries without distinguishing which were actually re-run in this session vs carried forward from the previous Miri run. At least one new file (scene, 47 tests) appears as 'Clean' but wasn't run under Miri in either the current or previous session based on the transcript skeleton.

The 'scene' test binary (47 tests) is listed as '47 pass, Clean' in the per-file table, but the scene library was created on 2026-03-13 (after the previous Miri run on 2026-03-11) and the worker transcript does not show running Miri on the scene binary. This entry may be unverified. Low risk since scene tests are pure drawing/layout logic unlikely to trigger Miri findings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-auditor skill's Miri section says 'run cargo +nightly miri test -- --test-threads=1' (blanket command), but the feature description and worker both correctly used per-binary approach. The skill should document the per-binary pattern explicitly since the blanket command is impractical for large test suites (exits on first UB per binary, doesn't give complete results).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Miri section: 'run cd system/test && cargo +nightly miri test -- --test-threads=1'. Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges confirms this gap. Feature description explicitly said 'Run ONLY per-test-binary, never the full suite.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-auditor skill doesn't mention Miri timeout handling or the cfg(miri) iteration reduction pattern for stress tests. Both are documented in the updated miri-compatibility.md but not in the skill where future workers would look for procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md has no mention of timeout limits or iteration reduction. miri-compatibility.md documents adversarial_slab at 90s and two skipped binaries exceeding 120s. scheduler_state already uses cfg(miri) iteration reduction as a pattern.

summary: PASS. The miri-analysis feature successfully ran Miri on all 4 new M1 bug-fix test files plus 8 additional critical files, found zero kernel UB, and produced a comprehensive miri-compatibility.md update including a detailed assembly-dependent blind spots section covering all 34 inline asm blocks. All 4 validation contract assertions (VAL-MIRI-001 through VAL-MIRI-004) are fulfilled. Four non-blocking documentation accuracy issues found: the header summary numbers (685 pass, 26 ignored, 31 verified) don't match the per-file table totals (893 pass, 27 ignored, 33 non-skipped), and at least one new file (scene) appears in the table as verified but wasn't actually run under Miri. These are precision issues in the summary statistics, not correctness issues with the Miri analysis itself.
